UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile on the Campus Pulse app.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user profile.

email.description: User's email address (must be @gla.ac.in for MVP).

transactionCount.description: Number of transactions the user has made today.

ItemListing.title: ItemListing
ItemListing.description: Represents an item listed for rent by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the item listing.

karma.description: Karma points awarded for renting the item.

available.description: Indicates if the item is currently available for rent.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Represents a rental transaction between two users.

fulfillerId.description: The UID of the user lending the item.

requesterId.description: The UID of the user borrowing the item.

itemId.description: Reference to the original ItemRequest ID.

itemName.description: Name of the item being transacted.

status.description: The current state of the transaction.

handoverCodeHash.description: The hashed 4-digit code for the initial handover.

handoverVerified.description: Whether the handover code has been successfully verified.

returnCodeHash.description: The hashed 4-digit code for the return.

returnVerified.description: Whether the return code has been successfully verified.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction was initiated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction was last updated.

ItemRequest.title: ItemRequest
ItemRequest.description: Represents a user's request for an item.

id.description: Unique identifier for the item request.

urgency.description: Urgency level of the request (Emergency, Medium, Normal).

requiredBy.description: The date and time the item is expected to be available.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. Uses path-based ownership.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores item listings. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' for authorization independence.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure authorization independence, clarity, and scalability. User profiles and item listings are stored in separate collections to optimize querying. Item requests are handled in a separate collection as well. Transactions are stored in a dedicated collection. To ensure authorization independence, the `ownerId` is stored within each `ItemListing` document, allowing rules to validate ownership without complex `get()` calls. Similarly, `requesterId` is stored in `ItemRequest`. Each `Transaction` stores `fulfillerId`, `requesterId`, and `itemId` for similar reasons.

The structure also facilitates secure list operations. By segregating data into collections based on entity type (users, items, requests, transactions), rules can be applied to entire collections rather than relying on document-level filtering. Using the dedicated collections approach alongside path-based ownership for private data and existence-over-content for global roles allows us to achieve Authorization Independence, DBAC, and support QAPs, thereby supporting secure list operations.
